string.text: Memory multiplier. (If NPC already saw target then NPC will receive boost to their vision against this target. Decreases with time, with default value of 1.0 it takes around 100 real-life seconds for NPC to drop their boost to 0 against this target) \n \n Default: 1.0
Distance multiplier. (Distance from object to object, range and fov) \n \n Default: 1.0
Makes some NPCs to use NVG instead of headlamp. NVG value is added regardless of distance, unlike headlamp ("0" disables feature). \n \n Default: 1
Enable the Danger Alert behavior for NPCs. Allows NPCs to react to friendly\hostile dangers. \n \n Default: Enabled
Enable the Combat AI Aim, which dynamically adjusts NPC aim angles, speed, and prediction based on conditions, weather, and NPC rank. \n \n Default: Enabled

Enables modified AI target selection logic. NPCs evaluate enemies using engine best_danger, memory data, and stored enemy state. Affects how combat targets are selected and updated during engagement. \n Default: Enabled

Enable NPCs to dynamically switch their weapons. NPCs will choose the most suitable weapon for combat. For example, if an NPC has a shotgun in their inventory, it will be used in close-range fights. \n \n Default: Enabled

Enable suicide behavior for NPCs of all factions. Triggers when NPC is critically wounded, surrendering, and near a visible enemy. If disabled, only Monolith NPCs follow the original vanilla behavior \n \n Default: Enabled
Enable NPCs to receive Medkits for use in combat. The number of Medkits is rank-based, with a minimum of 1 and a maximum of 3. WIP \n \n Default: Enabled
Disable Headlights in night combat for NPC. \n \n Default: Enabled
Prevents NPCs from entering dialogue while they are in combat. \n \n Default: Disabled
Enable NPC detection awareness. NPCs notice the player based on movement speed, carried weight, weather, flashlight use, and their own rank.\n \n Default: Enabled
Enable NPC foggy weather detection in pre-combat. NPCs will not engage in combat unless the distance threshold is reached. WIP.\n \n Default: Enabled
Enable NPC sniper combat detection when the player is using a sniper rifle. NPCs will switch to a camping scheme instead of direct engagement. WIP.\n \n Default: Enabled
Enable rank-based modifiers for NPC detection checks. \n \n Default: Enabled
Adjusts how much NPC rank affects detection checks. \n \n Default: 1.0
Enable weather-based modifiers for NPC detection checks. \n \n Default: Enabled
Adjusts how much weather affects detection checks. \n \n Default: 1.0

Enable NPC sound awareness in pre-combat. NPCs react to player and NPC sounds based on distance, type, weather, rank. Enable in-combat sound to allow NPCs to detect sounds during combat. \n \n Default: Enabled
Multiplier for NPC reaction to danger sounds (Weapon shoot\hit. Monster hit\attack). \n \n Default: 1.0
Multiplier for NPC reaction to weapon sounds (Weapon empty\reload\swith weapons.) \n \n Default: 1.0
Multiplier for NPC reaction to mutant sounds or other NPCs (Monster steps\talk\eat.). \n \n Default: 1.0
Multiplier for NPC reaction to item-related sounds (Item use\pickup\drop). \n \n Default: 1.0
Enable NPCs detect sounds in combat \n \n Default: Enabled
Multiplies NPC base sound detection range in combat. Higher values increase hearing sensitivity, lower values reduce it.\n \n Default: 1.0
Enable rank-based modifiers for NPC sound checks. \n \n Default: Enabled
Adjusts how much NPC rank affects sound checks. \n \n Default: 1.0
Enable weather-based modifiers for NPC sound detection. \n \n Default: Enabled
Adjusts how much weather affects NPC sound detection. \n \n Default: 1.0

CINEMATIC MODE: This behavior system restricts Combat AI activation to visual contact, preventing NPCs from engaging or maintaining combat states unless they can directly see their target. 
 
 NPCs will break out of camping and defensive behavior if they are exposed while lacking visual contact, ensuring consistent and realistic reactions based strictly on line of sight. 
 
 NPC behavior is influenced by distance thresholds, where different ranges control how actively NPCs move and respond; shorter distances result in more dynamic behavior, while longer distances tend to keep NPCs in static or camping states. The player visual contact with the NPCs is requaried for more advanced combat behavior. 
 
 When enabled, Combat AI logic is applied to NPC vs NPC engagements, whereas normally it is limited to player-related combat scenarios. 
 
 This creates more cinematic and immersive encounters, with all combat decisions driven by visibility, distance, and positioning. 
 
 VANILLA MODE: Combat AI logic is applied only during Player vs NPC engagements. 
 
 NPC vs NPC combat does not use Combat AI behavior, instead relying on more vanilla-like combat with general behavior improvements applied by the Combat AI system. 
 
 This keeps NPC interactions simpler while focusing advanced combat logic on the player.

Determines how much time NPCs get to move before returning to cover/camping behavior, affecting their perceived aggressiveness in combat. This time resets after each combat stimulus, such as gunfire or spotting an enemy. 
 This setting only changes movement time thresholds; the underlying coward, dumb, and stupid schemes remain unchanged, as do Faction/Rank-based attack behaviors. 
- AGGRESSIVE: NPCs have high movement thresholds, moving and taking action more often. 
- BALANCED: NPCs get moderate movement thresholds, alternating between advancing and holding position. 
- DEFENSIVE: NPCs have low movement thresholds, staying mostly in cover/camping and only moving when necessary. 
Default: Balanced

Enables AI combat positioning logic based on faction, rank, and weapon type. NPCs will attempt to maintain tactical distance when they lack direct line of sight to the enemy. This primarily affects mid-range and close combat behavior. \n Default: Enabled

Enables AI cover movement behavior. NPCs will relocate between cover positions during combat when they lack direct line of sight to the enemy. This behavior is primarily applied in mid-range and close-range engagements. \n Default: Enabled
Enable sprint movemets for the NPCs in combat \n \n Default: Enabled
Enable prone movemets for the NPCs to use prone position in combat. \n \n Default: Enabled
Enable NPCs to use rank- and faction-based wounded states in combat. This determines when NPCs become critically injured and lay down, affecting HP, wounded state. \n \n Default: Enabled

Controls how easily NPCs enter wounded states during combat. 
- RESILIENT: NPCs require more damage before becoming wounded. 
 
- BALANCED: Balanced Combat AI injury behavior. 
 
- VULNERABLE: NPCs require less damage before becoming wounded. 
 
Default: Balanced

Enables NPC panic behavior. NPCs may reposition in combat or flee to nearby smart terrain when health is low and combat pressure is unfavorable (based on rank, power ratio, and random morale factors). \n \n Default: Enabled
Enables squad panic behavior. Squads will flee to nearby smart terrain when combat pressure is high (based on rank, power ratio). \n \n Default: Enabled
Health (panic triggers when NPC health drops below threshold) (Only NPC Panic)
Radius (before panic starts, calculates enemies/friends power only in this radius)
Radius 2 (when already panicking, lower radius to cancel if weaker enemies nearby)
First panic seconds that can't be cancelled
First seconds after panic starts that can not be cancelled by weak enemy or hits
When NPC stops panicking, they can't panic again for this time
Pick a target to panic only if it's further than this value
Farthest NPC will cover their squad's panic
Mora's Combat ignore military fix v.2.2 Patch

In Cordon, Loners and Military maintain an uneasy ceasefire and avoid open conflict at a distance. Only close encounters or direct provocation will lead to fighting. Disable to restore the usual hostile behavior. This patch should be enabled with RE:DONE Collection Addon
Mora's NPCs without amnesia v3.0 Patch: This feature is built into Combat AI and is always enabled. It cannot be disabled and requires no action.
Recommended to enable this if you use Michiko's weather.

Displaying equation variables (for correct threshold you have to keep cursor on object all the time): 
 
 Console: all variables 
 
 Dynamic news: if object see actor or not 
 
 Bottom of the screen message: visibility threshold of object (and variables that you can change in MCM)

Displaying NPC ALERT state variables: 
 
 Console: npc name, stage, distance, see target and actor relation 
 
 Dynamic news: disabled 
 
 Bottom of the screen message: disabled

Displaying AI aim update information: 
 
 Console: AI aim update events 
 
 Console: NPC id, rank and rank value 
 
 Used to verify rank-based AI aim scaling

Displaying NPC self-healing information: 
 
 Console: NPC receiving medkits 
 
 Console: medkit and bandage usage 
 
 Console: healing and bleeding recovery events

Displaying NPC combat movement decisions: 
 
 Console: selected movement actions 
 
 Console: rank and weapon movement overrides 
 
 Used to verify combat movement behavior

Logging Detection System console prints:
 
 Console: [DETECTION] 
 
 Actor movement state detection 
 
 Weather, rain, fog and flashlight detection events

Logging SOUND / HEARING system console output:
 
 Console: [SOUND] 
 
 NPC hear callback processing (npc_on_hear_callback) 
 
 Sound classification: weapon, danger, item, movement, combat 
 
 Distance-based hearing detection with rank, weather and type multipliers 
 
 Combat sound reaction (noise radius в†’ state_mgr assault) 
 
 Danger propagation and visibility triggers

Logging META switch-table operations (NPC/ENEMY state caching): 
 Console: switch-table access / cache hit / init / cleanup events 
 Mode: RAW (all access) / CLEAN (only state changes) 
 Scope: NPC state tables, enemy state cache, priority handling 
 
 CLEAN mode removes duplicate logs for identical state transitions

Logging ENEMY selection & state transitions (NPC best_enemy system): 
 Console: ENGINE / MEMORY / SWITCH / STATE changes 
 Mode: RAW (all enemy resolution steps) 
 Mode: CLEAN (only enemy changes per NPC) 
 Scope: best_enemy(), db.storage.enemy_id, memory fallback 
 
 CLEAN mode filters repeated enemy assignments and only logs transitions 
 STATE -> NONE is logged when no valid enemy exists

Logging DANGER system state resolution (NPC best_danger + enemy assignment): 
 Console: ENGINE danger / HIT memory / MEMORY scan / ASSIGN / REJECT 
 Mode: RAW (all detection steps and evaluations) 
 Mode: CLEAN (only changes in assigned danger/enemy target per NPC) 
 Scope: npc:best_danger(), memory_visible_objects, hit tracking, st.enemy_id 
 
 CLEAN mode logs only when danger target changes or is invalidated 
 REJECT states include invalid class, dead objects, or non-enemy relations 
 ASSIGN events indicate confirmed valid danger target stored in NPC state
